rxn_class_summary: Electrocatalytic radical chlorotrifluoromethylative ene-yne cyclization of 1,6-enynes to chlorotrifluoromethylated pyrrolidines using anodically coupled electrolysis with Mn catalysis
electrochemical_role: Two parallel anodic oxidations on the carbon anode: (A) single-electron oxidation of CF3SO2- (from CF3SO2Na) with loss of SO2 to generate CF3 radical, and (B) single-electron oxidation of [Mn(II)]-Cl (formed from Mn(OTf)2 + MgCl2) to a persistent [Mn(III)]-Cl species that serves as a Cl-atom transfer reagent

key_intermediates.name: sp3 carbon-centered radical I after CF3 addition to alkene of 1,6-enyne
alkenyl (vinyl) radical II/IV after 5-exo-dig cyclization onto alkyne

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes CF3SO2- (-SO2) to CF3 radical, which adds regioselectively to the alkene of the 1,6-enyne to give a tertiary C-radical; this radical undergoes 5-exo-dig cyclization onto the tethered alkyne to give an alkenyl radical that is captured by anodically generated [Mn(III)]-Cl via Cl atom transfer, releasing [Mn(II)]-Cl which is re-oxidized at the anode; protons are reduced to H2 at the Pt cathode.
rate_determining_step: Cl-atom transfer from [Mn(III)]-Cl to the alkenyl radical (stereodetermining; under Curtin-Hammett control for alpha-alkyl alkenyl radicals)

probe_reagents.name: no Mn catalyst control
probe_reagents.observation: no desired pyrrolidine formed (entry 5/6), demonstrating essential role of Mn

probe_reagents.name: 2,2'-bipyridine ligand
probe_reagents.observation: adding 20 mol% bpy increased Z/E from 5.1:1 to >19:1, supporting steric control at Mn during Cl transfer

probe_reagents.name: KBr substitution for MgCl2
probe_reagents.observation: bromotrifluoromethylated pyrrolidine 19 obtained as single geometric isomer, consistent with analogous [Mn]-Br atom transfer

probe_reagents.name: X-ray of product 7
probe_reagents.observation: confirmed Z-configuration of alkene, supporting stereochemical model

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of CF3SO2Na and Mn(OTf)2/MgCl2
probe_reagents.observation: Eox(CF3SO2-) = 0.81 V and Eox([Mn(II)]-Cl) = 0.75 V vs Fc+/0; [Mn(III)/Mn(II)]-Cl couple reversible, indicating persistence of Mn-Cl species

notes: Stereoselectivity rationalized by Curtin-Hammett model: for alpha-aryl alkenyl radicals (sp-hybridized due to conjugation), steric shielding by quaternary C directs [Mn(III)]-Cl approach from less congested face, giving Z-selectivity; for alpha-alkyl alkenyl radicals (sp2), pro-(Z) and pro-(E) rotamers interconvert and competing kinetics/thermodynamics erode selectivity. Side pathways noted for substrates 20 (direct alkene/alkyne difunctionalization) and 21 (labile alpha-O C-H abstraction by CF3 radical giving CF3H). 1,7-enyne extension to piperidine 12 shows lower E/Z selectivity due to ring conformational flexibility during Cl transfer.
